inventory_findings.message: Caller-supplied content_mode 'full' is not a valid spec D1 corridor value (valid: tutorial, how-to, troubleshooting, explanation, reference, glossary, meta); D1 metric corridor skipped, structural heuristics still evaluated. Closest fit for this post is 'explanation'.

Caller-supplied content_mode 'full' is not a valid spec D1 corridor value (valid: tutorial, how-to, troubleshooting, explanation, reference, glossary, meta); D1 metric corridor skipped, structural heuristics still evaluated. Closest fit for this post is 'explanation'.

findings.message: Vale Microsoft.Dashes fires on every spaced em-dash ' — '; project CLAUDE.md explicitly mandates this style, systematic config conflict.
findings.evidence: the most honest thing the profile says about how I work.
findings.suggested_resolution: Add 'Microsoft.Dashes: NO' to a blog-repo .vale.ini; do not remove the em-dashes.

findings.message: Vale Microsoft.Contractions: 'is not' should be 'isn't'; also a post-writing-style a-15 obligation.
findings.evidence: Five products against roughly fourteen supporting repos is not an accident

findings.message: Vale Microsoft.Quotes: punctuation outside the closing quotation mark in the kamerplanter paragraph.
findings.evidence: calls it "agricultural technology system for plant lifecycle management", and its topics
findings.suggested_resolution: Logical quoting of an external description is defensible; or move the comma inside.

findings.message: Abbreviation 'MCP' (Model Context Protocol) appears without expansion on first use in body prose.
findings.evidence: an MCP client that talks to my server
findings.suggested_resolution: Expand on first use: 'a Model Context Protocol (MCP) client'.

findings.message: Abbreviation 'HACS' used without expansion on first occurrence; audience B may not recognise it.
findings.evidence: It's a HACS-installable Home Assistant integration
findings.suggested_resolution: Gloss as the Home Assistant Community Store (HACS).

findings.message: Abkürzung 'MCP' beim ersten Vorkommen nicht aufgelöst.
findings.evidence: ein MCP-Client, der mit meinem Server spricht
findings.suggested_resolution: Beim ersten Vorkommen ausschreiben: 'Model Context Protocol (MCP)'.

findings.message: Abkürzung 'HACS' beim ersten Vorkommen nicht aufgelöst.
findings.evidence: Es ist eine über HACS installierbare Home-Assistant-Integration
findings.suggested_resolution: Glossieren als Home Assistant Community Store (HACS).

findings.message: LanguageTool DOPPELPUNKT_GROSS: nach dem Doppelpunkt vermeintlicher Hauptsatz. Auditor-Gegenprüfung: es folgt eine Apposition ('das eine unterstützende Repo …'), kein Hauptsatz — Kleinschreibung korrekt, voraussichtlich False Positive.
findings.evidence: ist das Schaufenster: das eine unterstützende Repo, dessen Ergebnis gelesen statt betrieben werden soll.
findings.suggested_resolution: Vermutlich False Positive; keine Änderung nötig.

findings.message: Opening sentence ~40 words; FRE 59.5 / FKGL 9.5 (corridor skipped). Corroborates author self-check a-2.
findings.evidence: If you open my GitHub profile and sort by "recently pushed", you get a list that looks scattered: a plant-care system, a Kubernetes home lab
reachy-mini-mcp is a Model Context Protocol server wrapping the Pollen daemon in REST, so its reader is one level more technical
Wer mein GitHub-Profil öffnet und nach „zuletzt gepusht" sortiert, bekommt eine Liste, die zerstreut wirkt
findings.suggested_resolution: Nach dem Doppelpunkt neuen Satz beginnen.

findings.evidence: kamerplanter ist ein Plant-Lifecycle-System — die Repo-Beschreibung nennt es „agricultural technology system …"
Jedes Produkt sitzt auf einem Stapel von Repos, die nie einem Nutzer begegnen.

findings.message: Abbreviation 'REST' without gloss; audience B may not know it.
findings.evidence: wrapping the Pollen daemon in REST

findings.message: Abkürzung 'REST' ohne Glossierung; für Publikum B möglicherweise unklar.
findings.evidence: der den Pollen-Daemon in REST verpackt

findings.message: Hidden prerequisite for audience B: 'ESPHome YAML' without a gloss.
findings.evidence: a smart-home tinkerer who already writes ESPHome YAML and wants building blocks
findings.suggested_resolution: Add a short parenthetical gloss for ESPHome.

findings.message: Vale.Terms: 'esphome' inside a quoted repo description should be 'ESPHome'.
findings.evidence: described as "small reusable parts for works with esphome"
findings.suggested_resolution: Verbatim quote of upstream wording; leave as-is or note as quote.

findings.message: Microsoft.HeadingColons: word after colon not capitalized. Conflicts with CLAUDE.md sentence-case heading rule.
findings.evidence: ## The products: built for someone else to run
findings.suggested_resolution: Project sentence-case convention wins; rule conflict, decide at repo config level.

findings.evidence: ## The supporting cast: built so the products can ship
